quick_facts.label: Award and focus
quick_facts.value: B.Com (Bachelor of Commerce) is an undergraduate commerce qualification. Its course content commonly centres on accounting, finance, taxation, business/companies law, economics and related business skills; exact paper titles and electives are set by the university.

quick_facts.label: Entry qualification
quick_facts.value: Admission is generally after Class 12 (10+2) or an accepted equivalent, but required subjects, minimum marks and selection rules vary by institution. For example, one CUET-UG participating institution lists 10+2 with Commerce, Economics or Mathematics for B.Com.

quick_facts.label: Usual duration
quick_facts.value: The canonical B.Com route is a three-year undergraduate degree, although four-year undergraduate commerce pathways also operate at some universities under newer frameworks. Applicants should verify the specific programme structure rather than assume that all B.Com offerings have the same duration.

quick_facts.label: National entrance route
quick_facts.value: CUET-UG is an admission test used for undergraduate admissions by Central Universities and participating State, Deemed and Private Universities; a CUET score does not replace the individual university’s programme-specific eligibility requirements.

quick_facts.label: Professional-course alignment
quick_facts.value: After B.Com, a commerce graduate with at least 55% marks may register for ICAI CA Intermediate through the Direct Entry Route; ICMAI also lists a degree from a recognised university as a route for direct admission to CMA Intermediate.

sections.heading: Who can pursue B.Com?
paragraphs: B.Com admission normally follows successful completion of Class 12 (10+2) or an equivalent school-leaving qualification. There is no single all-India B.Com eligibility rule: universities specify their own subject combinations, aggregate-mark thresholds, reservation rules and admission processes.

Commerce in Classes 11–12 is a natural preparation, but it is not universally compulsory. Programme requirements can differ even among CUET-UG participating institutions: for example, the published B.Com requirement of one participant accepts Commerce, Economics or Mathematics at 10+2, while other institutions may require or prefer Accountancy, Business Studies, Mathematics, a language test, or specified CUET subjects.

Applicants should treat the target university’s current admission bulletin and programme page as controlling, particularly where a B.Com (Programme), B.Com (Hons.), finance/accounting specialisation, or four-year honours/research pathway is involved.
bullets: Class 12 eligibility, required subjects and minimum percentages are institution-specific.
A commerce background is useful preparation for the degree’s subject matter, but applicants must check whether it is mandatory at their target institution.
CUET-UG participation does not create a uniform B.Com subject or marks rule across universities.

sections.heading: Duration and study format
paragraphs: B.Com is conventionally a three-year undergraduate degree, normally organised across six semesters. That remains an important route in Indian higher education, as reflected in university examination listings for three-year B.Com and B.Com (Hons.) courses.

Duration now needs to be checked institution by institution. Universities implementing four-year undergraduate structures may also offer four-year commerce routes, with programme and exit options determined by their regulations. The degree title, honours designation, credit requirements and exit rules can therefore differ from a conventional three-year B.Com.
bullets: Expect a three-year B.Com in the conventional format.
Do not infer a programme’s duration solely from its title: confirm whether the offer is a three-year programme or a four-year undergraduate commerce route.
Check semester, credit, attendance, internship and exit requirements in the university’s current prospectus.

sections.heading: What students study
paragraphs: The curriculum is university-specific, but B.Com is designed around commerce and business foundations. A current University of Delhi B.Com programme structure, for example, includes core study in Income Tax Law and Practice and uses a credit-based mix of discipline-specific, skill, ability-enhancement, value-addition and elective courses.

This makes B.Com a useful academic base for accounting and compliance-oriented work as well as later study in finance, taxation, management or law. However, a B.Com by itself is an academic degree, not a licence to practise as a Chartered Accountant, Cost Accountant or Company Secretary; those professional designations require completion of their respective institute pathways.
bullets: Typical subject areas include financial accounting, corporate accounting, taxation, business law, economics, management and finance.
Electives, practical components, software exposure and specialisations vary materially by university.
Review the semester-wise syllabus before choosing between B.Com programme, honours and specialised variants.

sections.heading: Admission routes and how to apply
paragraphs: For many universities, admission is through CUET-UG followed by the relevant university’s counselling, preference-filling or seat-allocation process. NTA’s CUET-UG information bulletin governs the examination process, while each participating university publishes the B.Com eligibility, accepted CUET subjects, merit/selection method and admission schedule for its own programmes.

Other institutions may admit through their own entrance process or through Class 12 merit, subject to their rules. Consequently, applicants should first identify the exact B.Com variant and campus, then match their Class 12 subjects to the required test combination and submit applications through the institution’s official admissions portal.

Admission calendars, application fees, subject mappings, cut-offs and seat matrices are time-sensitive. They should be checked for the relevant admission cycle rather than taken from older notices or coaching-site summaries.
bullets: Read the current CUET-UG bulletin if applying through CUET.
Check the target university’s B.Com page for eligible CUET subjects and its counselling process.
Keep Class 10/12 records, category certificates where applicable, photographs and identity documents ready as required by the admitting institution.

sections.heading: Common progression routes after B.Com
paragraphs: B.Com can lead directly to entry-level accounting, finance, tax, operations and administrative roles, but many students combine it with a professional qualification or postgraduate entrance route. The best choice depends on whether the intended work is statutory audit and accountancy, cost and management accounting, company-law compliance, investments, management, teaching or law.

For CA, ICAI states that commerce graduates/postgraduates with at least 55% marks can use the Direct Entry Route to CA Intermediate; final-year students may register provisionally, subject to the stated conditions. For CMA, ICMAI’s prospectus identifies a recognised university degree as an eligibility route for direct admission to CMA Intermediate.

For CS, ICSI has announced CSEET restructuring effective from the June 2026 session and published information that graduates/postgraduates can receive an exemption from CSEET and enter CS Executive directly, subject to the applicable Institute conditions and fees. Applicants should use the current ICSI Executive registration notice because course and exemption rules can change.

For management, CAT is a common gateway to IIM and other management programmes. As dated CAT-2025 context, the official CAT bulletin required a bachelor’s degree with at least 50% marks or equivalent CGPA, with 45% for SC/ST/PwD candidates; individual institutes additionally apply their own selection criteria. For investment analysis, CFA Institute permits a completed bachelor’s degree holder to register for Level I, subject to its current registration and identification requirements.

B.Com is also a qualifying undergraduate base for further academic/professional study such as M.Com, three-year LL.B. where the law university’s graduate-entry rules are met, and B.Ed. where the relevant teacher-education admission rules are met. NCTE’s published draft 2025 regulations describe a two-year B.Ed. and 50% eligibility in a three- or four-year bachelor’s degree, but applicants should confirm the final applicable regulations and state/university admissions policy.
bullets: CA Intermediate: direct entry is available to eligible commerce graduates with the ICAI-prescribed minimum of 55%.
CMA Intermediate: a recognised university degree is listed by ICMAI as a direct-admission eligibility route.
CS Executive: verify the current CSEET-exemption and Executive-registration rules directly with ICSI.
CAT/MBA: a B.Com is an eligible bachelor’s discipline; CAT score alone does not guarantee admission to a management programme.
CFA Program: B.Com graduates may meet the degree-based registration requirement for Level I; CFA fees, exam windows and passport requirements should be verified at registration.
B.Ed., LL.B. and M.Com: availability and eligibility are set by the receiving university/regulator and can vary by state and institution.
